rag_rules: Fix AGREE diagnostics by resolving missing EOF errors in annex blocks, ensuring valid syntax for assume/guarantee contracts.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers in AGREE code that are not present in the AADL model or its dependencies.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE contracts; avoid invalid references or undefined identifiers.
Ensure AGREE scalar type declarations use correct names (int, real, bool) to prevent resolution errors.
Place implementation-level bindings (assign) only in implementation annexes, not in component type annexes.
When generating contracts, ensure guarantees relate visible inputs to visible outputs, avoiding implementation-only paths.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within AADL component annexes.
Assign statements in AGREE should reference valid output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in component type annexes that reference implementation-only subcomponent paths.
Do not introduce undefined or unqualified identifiers in AGREE contracts without proper AADL scope resolution.
